---
title: Greenwood
toc: false
---

**Modern survival analysis for Python.**

Greenwood is a time-to-event modeling library built for 2026: it works on pandas, Polars,
PyArrow, and anything else [narwhals](https://narwhals-dev.github.io/narwhals/) supports;
it is validated to tolerance against R's `survival` package; it visualizes with
[plotnine](https://plotnine.org/); and it plugs into the Great Tables ecosystem for
publication-quality tables. The name is a nod to Greenwood's formula, the classic variance
estimator for the Kaplan-Meier curve.

## Status

This foundational release provides the `Surv` response object (right, left, interval, and
counting-process censoring, with left truncation and multi-state endpoints) and the
narwhals-native risk-set kernel that Kaplan-Meier, the log-rank test, and Cox build on. The
estimators arrive in later releases; see the
[roadmap](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/ROADMAP.md).

## A first look

```python
import greenwood as gw
from greenwood import Surv

# Load a bundled dataset and build a response (lung codes 1 = censored, 2 = dead).
df = gw.data.load_dataset("lung")
y = Surv.right(df["time"], event=(df["status"] == 2))

# Tabulate the risk set at each event time (the kernel behind every estimator).
gw.event_table(y).to_dataframe().head()

# Coming soon:
# gw.KaplanMeier().fit(y, by=df["sex"])
# gw.logrank_test(y, group=df["sex"])
# gw.CoxPH().fit("Surv(time, status) ~ age + sex", data=df)
```
